title: Florida Is Paying $10,000 to Hunt Pythons. The Snakes Are Winning.
script: Florida losing its snake war? The state is about to launch its annual Python Challenge offering ten thousand dollars for the most pythons caught, and it still cannot dent a population estimated in the hundreds of thousands. Last year's winner caught 294 snakes in ten days. The Burmese python has no natural predators, breeds twice a year, and has eaten through 90 percent of the small mammal population in parts of the Everglades. Meanwhile, one alligator in Shark Valley last week settled the size debate for free: a gator dragged a 24-foot Burmese python across a trail in broad daylight in front of tourists. The python was the longest ever documented in the wild. The alligator needed no prize money. Can 10,000 hunters do what one alligator just did for free?
